title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Acceptance Evidence
summary: A team says “build a maze solver” without defining the player task, puzzle rules, assistance, supported input, repository state, or proof of success. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the acceptance evidence increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Separate pure-model, algorithm, Tcl interpreter, Tk widget, display, input, accessibility, package, and player evidence.
Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_outcomes_repo_accessibility_3(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Acceptance Evidence evidence · Theory Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-acceptance-evidence-jksl6s" that uses case 1102 to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-acceptance-evidence-jksl6s
# Competency: maze-acceptance-evidence.
# Case 1102: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_acceptance_evidence_jksl6s(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jksl6s = "jksl6s-s6lskj-1102"
    assert evidence_variant_jksl6s
    evidence_axes_jksl6s = (
        "gridjksl6s",
        "topology1102",
        "searchs6lskj",
        "eventjksl6s1102",
        "access1102jksl6s",
        "recoverys6lskj1102",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jksl6s)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
